title: Level 797

{{Background|file=Śnieg1.jpg}}{{Glacier Theme}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 797: "Alpine Village"}}{{Class 3|class=1e - Environmental|hexagon=
❆
|safety=Safe|security=Secure|entity=Mild Environmental Hazards|color-h=-160|color-s=75%}} Level 797 is the 798th level of the Backrooms. It was discovered on 29 November 1997. == Description == Level 797 appears as a mountainous area covered with ubiquitous snow, which significantly hinders movement and gives this place a special atmosphere. The size of the level itself is estimated at approximately 41,000 square kilometers. It is comparable to the country of Switzerland in the Frontrooms. Determining the exact area of this place is a challenging task due to the peaks reaching almost 9,000 meters in the border areas. The few daredevils who undertook the difficult climb over the dangerous peaks reported that they saw another mountain range behind them, beyond which large bodies of water were visible. Based on this information, it was concluded that the entire level is surrounded by an unexplored body of water.{{blurimage|file=Śnieg1.jpg|caption=One of the first pictures of this level.}}The level has a cycle of day and night, but due to the constant and prevailing winter season, the nights are much longer, lasting fifteen hours compared to nine hours of daylight. The level itself is characterized by frigid temperatures reaching up to -80°C in the highest mountain areas. Another difficulty is frequent snowfall, which results in avalanches, and these can end the lives of unlucky wanderers. === ''The Expedition'' === On April 14, 2004, a group of seventeen hikers decided to embark on an expedition to explore the farthest mountain range and what lay beyond. This expedition was largely funded by M.E.G., which wanted to gain even more information about this place. They hired two guides from the largest human settlement and set off. For the first few days of the expedition, they encountered nothing of note. They stopped at nearby settlements or outposts to wait out the frosty night. However, everything soon changed. ==== Recovered Note: ==== ''The following note was found at Outpost #23, located at the foot of the farthest mountain range.'' {{Note|title=We reached this outpost after twenty days of hiking from Lusenau. I didn't think it could be this damned cold here. Several of us already have severe frostbite, and Spencer is the worst. We had to amputate five of his fingers when we got here. I don't know why I volunteered to explore these remote areas. M.E.G. wasn't paying much, and I foolishly agreed, and now we're sweltering in this little wooden hut. Luckily, it has a decent supply of firewood, so the temperature isn't so bad. Our guide says that if we want to reach the peak at the foot of which we were currently standing, we have to set off in a few hours. He started to threaten us with the threat of a blizzard. I don't know what could be worse...staying here or freezing to death while climbing, trying to please those damn officials...}}{{Blurimage|file=Pexels-eberhardgross-746597.jpg|caption=Outpost #23}} ''The rest of the page is scorched, making it impossible to read the rest of the traveler's manuscript. The expedition itself disappeared completely, and no one ever found the frozen bodies or any remains. Local residents have several theories about what might have happened to them. Some claim they froze to death and their bodies are buried deep in the snow, while others assume they reached the icy waters and somehow drifted away from shore. Despite this, nothing is known for sure, and only a few people have made it to Outpost #23 since that expedition.'' === '''Mountain Peaks''' === Throughout the level, there are many mountain peaks, including nine-thousanders, which also form the conventional boundary of the level. However, most of them do not reach such heights, so wanderers usually have to climb three-thousanders if they want to overreach another mountain valley. The highest peak that has been conquered is Mount Rakmusz. The daredevils who achieved this belonged to the Rakmuszans, who named the peak in honor of their leader, and its height was estimated at 7,985 meters above sea level. A known fact is that they placed a small box with a notebook there, in which people who had conquered the summit could sign their names.{{blurimage|file=Obraz 2025-06-15 122719124.png|caption=Mount Rakmusz.}}Most mountains have their glaciers, which can make hiking significantly more difficult. This is due to the possibility of falling into an ice crevasse. This usually results in the death of the hiker due to the depth of these crevasses. Due to frequent snowfall, hiking on unmarked trails is not recommended. === '''Valleys''' === Valleys are the best areas to live on this level, even though they constitute a small part of the level in terms of area. They are most often found at altitudes between 500 and 2,000 meters above sea level. Coniferous trees also grow there, which are the main source of building materials and fuel for the inhabitants of this level. The largest of these is the Lusenau Valley, through which flows a river of the same name, which never freezes despite the freezing climate, all because it is fed by hot springs flowing from the surrounding peaks. It is also home to the largest human settlement on the level, which serves as a trading point and place of residence. The exact number of valleys on the level is unknown due to their quantity, but only eighteen of them are inhabited. Most of them are small communities, but they offer temporary shelter. === '''Caves''' === Caves can sometimes be found among mountain valleys. Most are not particularly large, but there are a few that are quite significant. Among these is the one near Lusenau, where an underground lake of hot water is located, used as a place of relaxation, rest, and even for treating superficial wounds that hikers may incur during their journeys. == Entities == Due to the low temperatures, only a small number of beings have been recorded here, the most common being [[Entity 3|Smilers]] and [[Entity 8|Hounds]]. Moreover, the presence of [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 47|Mushroom Maws]], and Krampus has also been reported. === '''Ice Knights''' === It has been reported that during snowstorms, some wanderers have observed humanoid figures watching them from a distance. Rakmuszan expeditions have confirmed that this is not a being that only exists on this level. During one such mission, they managed to get near to this phenomenon, which provided significant information on the subject. It was discovered that these are ice figures about two meters tall. The ice itself is transparent, and elements of knightly armor are engraved on its surface. No life activity has been detected in these figures, which is why they are considered a special creation of this level. Despite this, many residents believe they are travelers who died during their mountain journeys. They believe that their level, to some extent, makes them protectors of the mountain ranges and a force that allows them to control the lives of the local people. Several attempts were made to bring these figures to one of the settlements. Only one was successful. After their arrival, the ice figure stood in the very center of the settlement, awaiting the arrival of a group of scientists who were to examine it. During this time, it was under heavy surveillance by guards tasked with preventing anyone from destroying or stealing the find. However, when the researchers reached the outskirts of the settlement, something inexplicable happened. {{Blurimage|file=Krampus.png|caption=Krampus}} The sky above the houses turned completely black, and a fierce, icy wind began to blow. This lasted several minutes before total disaster struck. A dark fog slowly engulfed house after house, turning everything into a block of ice. Anyone not quick enough was swallowed by it. Only a dozen residents and two researchers survived the incident. They then decided to enter the houses to see if anyone had survived. What they saw shocked them. All those unlucky enough to have escaped were now identical ice figures to the one brought here. The wood from which the buildings were constructed also turned to ice. The entire place became a kingdom of ice. After this, the survivors immediately evacuated to the nearest settlement, and M.E.G. dispatched several groups of agents to cordon off the area and prevent anyone from accessing it. From that point on, no further research was attempted on the ice figures, and they themselves were considered part of the site. === '''Krampus''' === It's difficult to say whether this entity truly exists. For many, it's merely a legend intended to frighten children into behaving well and not venturing out onto mountain trails alone without adult supervision. A small number, however, claim to have seen a humanoid figure with large horns traversing the mountainous wilderness with a large sack on its back. Rumors vary, but no photos were taken, and those who attempted it were immediately attacked by a snowstorm. However, the Rakmusans know that this creature periodically kidnaps unlucky individuals to place them in its large sack. What happens next is unknown. They claim that the sack contains a passage to the Void, and Krampus himself is part of it. == Colonies and Outposts == === '''Outposts''' === Scattered throughout the level, there are small buildings that can serve as shelters for exhausted or frozen wanderers.{{blurimage|file=Śnieg3.jpg|caption=One of the outposts.}} Usually, it is a single wooden building that can accommodate up to five people with all their equipment; as an exception, there are cases where the shelters are larger or consist of two or three buildings. However, this is seldom. Each place is equipped with a supply of firewood, which is restocked by the Rakmuszans, who also built this network of buildings. Of all the outposts, only two are permanently inhabited. This is due to their location and function. === '''Lusenau Settlement''' === This is the largest human settlement on this Level. As reported, it is estimated that the Lusenau settlement is inhabited by around a thousand people.{{blurimage|file=Śnieg4.jpg|caption=Lusenau buildings.}}Due to its location near hot springs, the landscape is ideal for living. In addition, geothermal waters have healing properties and can heal wounds on the human body duration within a few minutes of bathing. The inhabitants live in wooden huts built from coniferous trees growing on the plateau. It is also home to the most important trading point on this Level, where other wanderers go to sell or exchange their goods. The [[M.E.G.]] has established its branch here, mainly to help wanderers who have reached this level. === '''Other Significant Human Communities''' === ==== '''Rakmuszans Settlement''' ==== * Located in one of the valleys. Its exact location is unknown; * It is inhabited by about 400 people; * It is the main Rakmuszans settlement in the entire Backrooms. ==== '''Station #1''' ==== * Located at the foot of Mount Rakmusz; * It is inhabited by five people; * The inhabitants are responsible for maintaining the station and securing the surrounding mountain trails. ==== '''Station #2''' ==== * Located 15 kilometres north of the Lusenau Valley; * It consists of two wooden residential buildings * It is currently inhabited by one person. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * Attempting to reach outside in [[Level 330.1]] will eventually lead one to here after a long snowstorm; * Sinking into the snow at [[Level 76]] can sometimes transport one to this level; * Entering a hollow on [[Level 8]] may transport one to one of the huts in Lusenau Valley; * Jumping into the ocean on [[Level 7]] may at some cases transport one to the hot springs. * Any noclipping within an anomalously cold room in [[Level 0.8]] may send one here. === Exits === * Bathing in the Lusenau River for more than two hours will cause the wanderer to penetrate to [[Level 37]]; * Jumping off the top of Mount Rakmusz will cause the person to lose consciousness and then wake up on [[Level 72]].
